deadlines.title: FoundryIQ & Tools - Foundational Customer Interviews
deadlines.body:
Initial foundational customer interviews to understand:

- Agentic use cases
- Opportunities for data to ground agents: Use cases with knowledge/data/foundry IQ. 
- What format is their data in? _Where_ is their data?
- How are they connecting data to their agents today? Using Foundry? Other methods? Why/how did they make those decisions
- Pain points with connecting data to agents today (in Foundry and elsewhere)

This initial effort will focus on existing customers.

deadlines.title: Python Day 0 ethnographic benchmark
deadlines.body:
In 2021, an ethnographic benchmark was run to to create a baseline for an OKR to increase the number of new customers that successfully create a simple Python web app on App Service by 20%.  In March of 2023, we replicated the same ethnographic benchmark study with 10 Python users. Both the studies found that there were some improvements required. The Engineering team has not worked on below improvements:

1. Organized the tabs on the docs to make sure that customers land of the right tabs for their preferred tools. We also fixed a couple of issues in the Django source repo for our quickstarts.
2. Fixed the permission issues for github integration on the portal
3. Improvements in time-taken for deployments
4. Integration of the deployment status api in the portal, az cli and azd
5. Site status indicator on the portal

The team wants to rerun the study to validate if the issues have been resolved.

These are the timelines
16th Mar 2026 – Start the interviews. We will be starting the customer recruitment in another week.
1st week of April – share the findings

deadlines.title: Browser Automation Discovery
deadlines.body:
Browser Automation Tool in Foundry enables AI agents to reliably interact with real-world web applications in a secure, observable, and scalable way. 
As AI agents move beyond APIs into the open web, existing browser automation tools fall short on reliability, isolation, observability, and enterprise readiness. Our strategy is to build a browser automation tool that is agent-first, cloud-scale, and enterprise-grade.

deadlines.title: Agentic AI Builders – Show and Tell
deadlines.body:
**Description**: A recurring research series led by the UX research team with the goal of deepening our understanding of how developers and technical decision-makers are leveraging agentic AI workflows using Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) offerings on Azure.

**Links**:
- Study planner and initial screener: https://microsoft-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/p/tarynbipat/EUgJfWuSx-JLjMzV3j29Y9gB05t8qQx-0wv2FEu0yyir8Q?e=xIUkLz

deadlines.title: Evaluate product-market fit for SRE Agent heading towards GA
deadlines.body: SRE Agent has been in private preview since July, with hundreds of customers trialing the product. The team is balancing investments in agentic quality with the long tail of IT needs blocking customer adoption. Since this Preview is somewhat unusual in that customers are already being billed, the question of announcing GA is more about endorsing the current state of the product. While many people inside and outside Microsoft see transformative potential in Agentic DevOps, the devil is in the details: team has lingering questions about the efficacy of their product and its product-market fit. We also need to refine the team’s hygiene of listening to and learning from customers as they transition towards GA.

deadlines.title: Day 1 survey analysis
deadlines.body: Now that we have a pipeline to gather Day 1 data, the team is curious how satisfaction and trust are trending, and what dissatisfied customers are saying.

deadlines.title: Agentic DevOps customer segmentation
deadlines.body: With potential interest in a DevOps agent and increased interest from DevOps engineers in more DevOps-y capabilities of SRE Agent, we'll start hypothesizing a model of how we might think about the roles devs, DevOps engineers, and SREs play in a growing company's SDLC.

deadlines.title: AITK Foundry Extensions Study
deadlines.body:
AI Toolkit and Foundry are two VS Code extensions which serve overlapping parts of the AI development lifecycle. The distinct user, use case, and value of the two extensions are unclear to both the team and our customers. 
The central question: Where does AITK end and AI Foundry begin — and how should developers flow within/between them?

deadlines.title: Outset AI test - boundries
deadlines.body:
**This is a product test for ResOPs**

RQ:  What are the boundaries between those tasks (or task steps) that developers will willingly hand over to AI vs demanding to do themselves?  What motivates those boundaries (anxieties ?  experience ? self-efficacy?)

This study will use AI run interviews to learn more about this topic from participants (developers) who report using AI 25% of the time or more.

deployments.title: Validating the Value of Agent-Driven Testing
deployments.body:
App Testing team wants to validate if ALT tools should be exposed via Azure MCP.
Unlike traditional model, MCP adoption depends on 2 sided model:

- Agent builders who choose to integrate ALT MCP tools into their agents/platforms
- Agent end users who need to trust and use those capabilities to run load tests on their own apps.

This research is going to validate both sides of the adoption chain to ensure Azure invests in the right tools, exposed at the right surface, for the right personas.

deployments.title: Multi Agent Workflow Interviews + VS Code Agent Sessions Review
deployments.body: I interviewed three participants about their existing multi-agent workflows in semi-structured, one-hour sessions. Unlike our previous observational studies where participants completed tasks, these interviews focused on self-reported workflows: how developers actually structure, decompose, coordinate, and review work across multiple agent sessions in their real work environments. All three are experienced, active multi-agent users who have built sophisticated pipelines involving orchestration tools, specialized agent roles, and cross-tool coordination. Toward the end of each session, I showed participants a demo of the Agent Sessions Window in VS Code to capture their initial reactions and assess whether, at first glance, it felt like it addressed any of the workflow problems they had shared.

dependencies.title: Knowledge Base Churn Analysis
dependencies.body:
Analyze available data for churn within the Knowledge Base creation flow

Key parts of the user flow are:
- Begin to create Knowledge Base
- Successfully create Knowledge Base
- Use Knowledge Base with an agent

Today, only ~30% of those who start to create a KB go on to successfully create one.

Planned work:

- Exploratory data analysis to form (more) hypotheses
- Launch an intercept survey at a certain point in the flow to understand customers' intent and where/why they are falling off.

dependencies.title: GItHub Proxima Migration and Hybrid Experience.
dependencies.body:
Collecting feedback from repo migrators and day-to-day users on the impacts of GitHub Proxima "hybrid" workflows.

dependencies.title: PR Assistant - Opportunities for Improvement
dependencies.body:
PM: Atoshi Sahoo

Identify top users of PR Assistant (by way of high counts "fixed", indicating that the user actually responded to PR Assistant's suggestion).

We'll then interview those users about their experience, looking for opportunities to improve the product.

Finally, we'll also conduct a competitive analysis to determine how competitors address issues that are identified by our customer interviews.

dependencies.title: Comparison of Cross-Functional vs. Engineering-Only Squads Participating in Camp AIR
dependencies.body:
Partners: Thomas Verhasselt, Aparna Rao, and Dean Carignan

This study uses semi‑structured exit interviews to compare perceived outcomes and adoption patterns between 5 participants who attended Camp AIR as cross‑functional teams and 5 participants who attended as engineer‑only teams. 

Through a “slow down to speed up”, immersive, hands-on change experience, Camp AIR works with teams to redesign and automate workflows, encourage new habits, and create a culture that embraces an “AI-first” mindset.

[Research Plan](https://microsoft-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/r/personal/travisl_microsoft_com/Documents/Research/AI/Camp%20AIR/Camp%20AIR%20-%20Research%20Plan.docx?d=w3ddd52641f414173baf3fa78a6861111&csf=1&web=1&e=nLSX10)

dependencies.title: Observation study: unifying experience for agent development
dependencies.researcher: JP Carrascal, Yuie Yu (CSI INTERFUSION INC)
dependencies.body:
**Context and Background**
There is a fragmentation and overlap of agent SDKs, tools, and platforms within Microsoft (specifically between Teams, Microsoft 365, and AI Foundry), which leads to duplicated efforts and a lack of unified developer experience. This creates confusion for customers (AI engineers), making it difficult to identify a recommended path, select appropriate tools, and understand deployment options.


More info: https://microsofteur-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/jucarra_microsoft_com/EXVIUQ462bJKqljwmSyl0zwBe2LMp3jgto1-f0cZ0NWTvQ?e=td6hor

dependencies.title: VS Code Data Wrangler for AI/Agents
dependencies.body:
Currently in in planning
Goal is to identify gaps/opportunities in Data Wrangler feature in VSC for Agentic AI scenarios. 
Details: We'll be recruiting AI Engineers/DS (also open to other roles) who engage in data related JTBD's like exploration, manipulation and preparation for agentic/AI scenarios and who engage in data preparation for model fine tuning (stretch requirement), evaluations, or who compare evaluation results for AI/agentic scenarios (hard requirements). 
- Understand workflow, pain points, and opportunities related to data JTBD's in agentic/AI scenarios
- Observe use/engagement with Copilot + DW and capture feedback 
- Understand perspectives on customer success (what is considered "success", what competitors if any are doing a great job and why/how)
